title: Create a Virtual Machine (Console + gcloud)
aim: To create virtual machines using Google Cloud Console (GUI) and gcloud CLI, and deploy an NGINX web server.

tasks.title: Create a VM from the Cloud Console

steps.description: Navigate to Compute Engine > VM Instances in the Google Cloud Console.
Click 'Create Instance' at the top of the page.
In the Firewall section, check 'Allow HTTP traffic'.
Click 'Create' and wait for the instance to be provisioned.
In the VM Instances list, click 'SSH' next to the 'gcelab' instance.
Copy the External IP of 'gcelab' and paste it into a browser to see the 'Welcome to nginx!' page.
Open Cloud Shell by clicking the '>_' icon in the top right header.
Set the default region and zone variables (replace with your assigned zone).
steps.command:
gcloud config set compute/region [REGION]
export REGION=[REGION]
export ZONE=[ZONE]

steps.description: Create a new instance named 'gcelab2' using the gcloud CLI.
steps.command: gcloud compute instances create gcelab2 --machine-type e2-medium --zone=$ZONE

steps.description: Connect to your new instance via SSH from Cloud Shell.

commands.cmd: gcloud compute instances create gcelab2 --machine-type e2-medium --zone=$ZONE

viva.question: What is a VM?
viva.answer: A Virtual Machine (VM) is a compute resource that uses software instead of a physical computer to run programs and deploy apps.

viva.question: What is gcloud?
viva.answer: gcloud is the primary CLI tool for managing Google Cloud resources.

result: Successfully created VM instances using both the Console and gcloud CLI, and verified NGINX web server deployment.
conclusion: Learned how to provision compute resources on GCP using GUI and CLI methods.
